concepts.finding: Network embedding via truncated random walks treated as sentences. Applies Skip-gram to vertex sequences generated by random walks on graph. Outperforms spectral methods with 60% less training data. Learns label-independent representations capturing community structure.
concepts.implication: Keyword co-occurrence can be modeled as a graph where nodes are keywords, edges are co-occurrence weights. Random walks generate 'keyword sentences' that capture higher-order relationships beyond direct co-occurrence.
concepts.open_question: What edge weighting scheme best captures keyword relatedness for JTBD clustering?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: DeepWalk embeddings on a keyword co-occurrence graph will capture higher-order intent relationships that direct co-occurrence (word2vec) misses.
